issues.message: TS2305: Module '"@marigold/components"' has no exported member 'Box'.
issues.suggestion: Fix the TypeScript error reported by the compiler. The exact location is included in the issue location field.

issues.message:
TS2322: Type '(keys: Set<string> | "all") => void' is not assignable to type '(keys: Selection) => void'.
  Types of parameters 'keys' and 'keys' are incompatible.
    Type 'Selection' is not assignable to type 'Set<string> | "all"'.
      Type 'Set<Key>' is not assignable to type 'Set<string> | "all"'.
        Type 'Set<Key>' is not assignable to type 'Set<string>'.
          Type 'Key' is not assignable to type 'string'.
            Type 'number' is not assignable to type 'string'.
issues.suggestion: Fix the TypeScript error reported by the compiler. The exact location is included in the issue location field.

issues.message: <Grid> is used without any of its sub-components.
issues.suggestion: Use compound children: <Grid.Area>.

issues.message: <Inline> wraps a single child. A flow layout earns its place only with two or more children; with one child it adds a wrapper that does nothing.
issues.suggestion: Render the child directly instead of wrapping it in <Inline>. For single-child spacing use Inset, Center, Breakout or Aspect.

issues.message: <Table> has no column marked rowHeader. Exactly one Table.Column should set rowHeader so screen readers can announce each row by it.
issues.suggestion: Add rowHeader to the column that identifies each row, e.g. <Table.Column rowHeader>Name</Table.Column>.

issues.message: <Table> has no column marked rowHeader. Exactly one Table.Column should set rowHeader so screen readers can announce each row by it.

warnings.message: <Inline> wraps a single child. A flow layout earns its place only with two or more children; with one child it adds a wrapper that does nothing.
warnings.suggestion: Render the child directly instead of wrapping it in <Inline>. For single-child spacing use Inset, Center, Breakout or Aspect.

warnings.message: <Table> has no column marked rowHeader. Exactly one Table.Column should set rowHeader so screen readers can announce each row by it.
warnings.suggestion: Add rowHeader to the column that identifies each row, e.g. <Table.Column rowHeader>Name</Table.Column>.
